skill.name: usmle-case-generator
skill.description: Generate USMLE Step 1/2 style clinical cases with patient history, physical exam, labs, multiple-choice questions, and answer explanations for medical education.
skill.summary: Generate USMLE Step 1/2 style clinical cases with patient history, physical exam, labs, multiple-choice questions, and answer explanations for medical education.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 88 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'System reconnaissance' flags are caused by medical terminology (MD for Doctor of Medicine, Step for exam levels, physical exam findings) being misinterpreted by the pattern-based scanner. 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' flags are code examples inside markdown documentation blocks, not actual executable commands. 'Path traversal sequence' flags are from a security checklist documenting defensive practices. The skill is a legitimate medical education tool with hardcoded template data, no external network calls, and no credential exposure.

low_findings.title: External command references in skill documentation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains 40 code block examples with bash and Python commands showing how to invoke the skill. All commands are hardcoded documentation examples (e.g., 'python scripts/main.py --help'). These are not automated execution - they require explicit user action. No command injection risk exists since no dynamic input is passed to shell commands. FALSE POSITIVE - pattern is safe markdown documentation.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded GitHub source URL
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 7 contains a hardcoded URL pointing to the GitHub source repository. This is legitimate attribution and standard practice for open-source skills. No credential exfiltration or malicious endpoint detected - the URL points to the author's public medical research skills repository.

low_findings.title: Security checklist with path traversal references
low_findings.description: SKILL.md lines 224 and 227 mention '../' notation inside a security checklist section (items like 'No unauthorized file system access (../)' and 'Input file paths validated (no ../ traversal)'). These are documenting intended defensive practices, not actual path traversal code. FALSE POSITIVE.

low_findings.title: Misidentified medical terminology as cryptographic terms
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 44 instances of 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' across all files. These are false positives caused by medical terminology overlap (e.g., 'MD' for Doctor of Medicine mistaken for Message Digest, 'Step' for exam level, medical abbreviations, and dosage references). All flagged lines contain medical education content - none contain actual cryptographic code.

low_findings.title: Misidentified reconnaissance terminology
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 12 instances of 'System reconnaissance' and 2 instances of 'Network reconnaissance'. These are false positives from medical examination terminology (e.g., 'physical examination findings', 'fundoscopic examination', 'laboratory studies', 'network' referring to neural networks or referral networks in medical context). All flagged lines are medical education content.

content.user_title: Generate USMLE Clinical Cases for Medical Exam Preparation
content.value_statement: Medical students need structured practice cases to prepare for USMLE Step 1 and Step 2 exams. This skill generates realistic clinical vignettes with multiple-choice questions and detailed explanations to improve diagnostic reasoning and board exam performance.
actual_capabilities: Generates Step 1 cases focused on pathophysiology and basic science mechanisms
Generates Step 2 CK cases focused on clinical diagnosis and management decisions
Creates complete patient vignettes with history, physical exam, vital signs, labs, and imaging
Provides USMLE-style single best answer questions with five answer options
Includes detailed answer explanations with rationale and learning objectives
Supports multiple output formats including plain text, JSON, and markdown
limitations: Cases are generated from fixed template data, not from real patient records
Medical content should be verified against authoritative sources before use
Does not replace professional medical education or clinical judgment
Condition database is limited to common high-yield topics covered by USMLE

use_cases.title: Step 1 Exam Review
use_cases.description: A medical student generates Step 1 cases focused on pathophysiology and basic science to reinforce classroom learning and identify knowledge gaps in specific topics.
use_cases.target_user: Medical student preparing for USMLE Step 1

use_cases.title: Clinical Reasoning Practice
use_cases.description: A resident or clinical student practices differential diagnosis and management decisions using Step 2 cases that test clinical decision-making and next best steps.
use_cases.target_user: Resident or medical student in clinical rotations

use_cases.title: Teaching Material Creation
use_cases.description: A medical educator generates structured clinical cases for small group discussions, problem-based learning sessions, or classroom teaching with consistent formatting.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Basic Step 2 Case
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a USMLE Step 2 clinical case about cardiology
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants a general cardiology case for practice.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Step 1 Pathophysiology Case
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a Step 1 case about asthma with a focus on disease mechanisms
prompt_templates.scenario: A second-year medical student needs a basic science case for pathophysiology review.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Specific Condition Case
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a Step 2 case about diabetic ketoacidosis at medium difficulty
prompt_templates.scenario: A student needs to practice a specific high-yield condition with moderate complexity.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Multiple Cases for Study Group
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate 3 different Step 2 cases covering cardiology, pulmonology, and nephrology. Include answer explanations. Output in markdown format.
prompt_templates.scenario: A study group needs multiple cases for a group session with formatted output.

output_examples.input: Generate a Step 2 case about pulmonary embolism
output_examples.output: A 45-year-old female presents with sudden onset shortness of breath and pleuritic chest pain after an 8-hour flight. Vitals show tachycardia. Physical exam reveals right calf swelling. Labs show elevated D-dimer. The output includes a complete clinical vignette with history, exam findings, diagnostic results, a USMLE-style multiple choice question, and a detailed explanation of the correct management approach.

output_examples.input: Generate a Step 1 case about multiple sclerosis
output_examples.output: A 28-year-old female presents with painful vision loss in the right eye and a history of transient leg weakness. MRI shows periventricular white matter lesions. The case focuses on pathophysiology with a question about disease mechanisms, five answer options, and a detailed explanation covering autoimmune demyelination and immunology concepts.

output_examples.input: Generate a Step 2 case about acute pancreatitis at hard difficulty
output_examples.output: A 45-year-old male with gallstones presents with severe epigastric pain radiating to the back after heavy alcohol use. Labs show elevated lipase and amylase. The output includes a challenging clinical scenario requiring nuanced management decisions, complete with diagnostic studies, treatment options, and a detailed evidence-based explanation.

best_practices: Specify the USMLE step level (Step 1 or Step 2) and medical topic to get targeted practice cases that match your study goals
Review generated cases against authoritative medical references before using them in high-stakes exam preparation settings
Use the JSON output format when integrating cases into custom study applications or flashcard systems
anti_patterns: Do not use generated cases as a substitute for real clinical experience or direct patient interaction in medical training
Do not assume that case difficulty rating matches actual USMLE exam difficulty without independent verification
Do not rely on a single topic set for comprehensive review since the condition database covers only common high-yield conditions

faq.question: What USMLE steps does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports Step 1 (basic science and pathophysiology) and Step 2 CK (clinical knowledge and management) case generation.

faq.question: Which medical topics are available for case generation?
faq.answer: Cardiology, pulmonology, gastroenterology, nephrology, endocrinology, infectious disease, and neurology are currently supported.

faq.question: Can I request a specific medical condition?
faq.answer: Yes, you can specify conditions such as diabetic ketoacidosis, pulmonary embolism, acute myocardial infarction, and many other high-yield topics.

faq.question: What output formats are available?
faq.answer: Three formats are available: plain text for reading and printing, JSON for application integration, and markdown for documentation or web display.

faq.question: How many cases can I generate at once?
faq.answer: You can specify a count from 1 to 10 cases in a single request for study sessions or group practice.

faq.question: Are the generated cases medically accurate?
faq.answer: Cases follow standard USMLE patterns and evidence-based guidelines but should be reviewed by a medical professional before use in educational settings.
